title: xz-utils backdoor
subtitle: liblzma supply-chain implant
query: Assess CVE-2024-3094 in xz-utils / liblzma and tell me how urgently to act.

data.query: Assess CVE-2024-3094 in xz-utils / liblzma and tell me how urgently to act.

data.summary: CVE-2024-3094 is a deliberate supply-chain backdoor (CVSS 10.0 Critical) in xz-utils/liblzma 5.6.0–5.6.1. The tampered library intercepts linked software — notably OpenSSH — enabling potential unauthenticated RCE. EPSS is 86% probability at the 99.7th percentile and public detection PoCs exist.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Attend (near Act) — 86% EPSS, public exploits, CVSS 10.0, not yet on KEV. Immediately: (1) check `xz --version`; (2) downgrade to xz-utils ≤5.4.x via your distro's emergency package; (3) restart sshd; (4) audit SSH logs for anomalous auth. Follow your distro advisory for the exact fixed package version.

cves.summary: Malicious code deliberately embedded in xz-utils/liblzma 5.6.0–5.6.1 via supply-chain compromise. Tampered liblzma intercepts linked software (notably OpenSSH), enabling potential unauthenticated RCE.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-3094) → CVSS 10.0 Critical, CWE-506 (Embedded Malicious Code), affects xz 5.6.0 and 5.6.1 only
exploit_check(CVE-2024-3094) → has_public_exploit=true; GitHub PoC detection scripts found
kev_check(CVE-2024-3094) → in_catalog=false; not on CISA KEV
epss_score(CVE-2024-3094) → probability=0.860, percentile=0.997 — extremely high exploitation likelihood
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-3094) → has_fix=false in NVD CPE data; distro rollback to ≤5.4.x is the remediation path
attack_mapping([CWE-506]) → no curated ATT&CK mapping returned for CWE-506
Decision: SSVC Attend/near-Act — public exploit + 86% EPSS + Critical CVSS; immediate downgrade required

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Act: CVE-2024-3094 a public exploit is available and EPSS predicts high near-term exploitation.
